2603.19595.title: All-Mem: Agentic Lifelong Memory via Dynamic Topology Evolution
2603.19595.paper_summary: Lifelong interactive agents are expected to assist users over months or years, which requires continually writing long term memories while retrieving the right evidence for each new query under fixed context and latency budgets. Existing memory systems often degrade as histories grow, yielding redundant, outdated, or noisy retrieved contexts. We present All-Mem, an online/offline lifelong memory framework that maintains a topology structured memory bank via explicit, non destructive consolidation, avoiding the irreversible information loss typical of summarization based compression. In online operation, it anchors retrieval on a bounded visible surface to keep coarse search cost bounded. Periodically offline, an LLM diagnoser proposes confidence scored topology edits executed with gating using three operators: SPLIT, MERGE, and UPDATE, while preserving immutable evidence for traceability. At query time, typed links enable hop bounded, budgeted expansion from active anchors to archived evidence when needed. Experiments on LOCOMO and LONGMEMEVAL show improved retrieval and QA over representative baselines.
2603.19595.paper_summary_zh: 終身互動代理預期將在數月或數年內協助使用者，這需要持續寫入長期記憶，同時在固定的上下文和延遲預算下為每個新查詢檢索正確的證據。現有的記憶系統隨著歷史的增長往往會退化，導致檢索到的上下文冗餘、過時或雜訊。我們提出了All-Mem，一個在線/離線的終身記憶框架，通過明確的、非破壞性的整合來維持結構化的記憶庫，避免了總結壓縮所典型的不可逆信息損失。在在線操作中，它將檢索固定在一個有限的可見表面上，以保持粗略搜索成本的上限。定期離線時，一個LLM診斷器提出信心分數的拓撲編輯，這些編輯通過使用三個運算子：SPLIT、MERGE和UPDATE進行門控執行，同時保留不可變的證據以便追溯。在查詢時，類型鏈接使得從活動錨點到存檔證據的跳躍有界、預算化的擴展成為可能。對LOCOMO和LONGMEMEVAL的實驗顯示出相對於代表性基準的檢索和QA的改進。
2603.19595.author: Can Lv et.al.
2603.19595.authors: Can Lv, Heng Chang, Yuchen Guo, Shengyu Tao, Shiji Zhou
